item.title: Some Programming Humor For A Niche Audience (BLESSED I.T. Sunday)
item.description:
1. People here are told their iPhone is upgraded (it's not) I know a lot of people here are also iPhone users, but I feel they wouldn't fall for that. The average person on the street tho....
2. Yeahhhh
3
4. O shit
5
6
7. Pure chaos
8
9
10
11
12. Seriously what is Scroll Lock even for. I've never used it, I think just for one single flight sim game.
13. Network might be a little slow today
14. QA at it again...
15. Thank you if you made this far in the dump! Let me share with you that as well as posting on Imgur, I'm also working on a game of my own. A passion project, if you will. It's a "Stanley Parable" inspired first person narrative game. If you like the humor of this dump, you might actually like the game to (at least what my friend on Discord tells me, that one friend). Anyway please wishlist it helps small game devs like me: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ P.S. 1 Here is the latest addition - The Humorless Toaster. It's only here to make toast. It doesn't have time for your nonsense. P.S. 2 DM me some memes P.S. 3 If you have some to spare P.S. 4 Is that OKAY? P.S. 5. PRO. NO DISC DRIVE, NO VERTICAL STAND, $699
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: Aditi Shankar received the UK?s first rejection free kidney ? and it was given by her mum!
item.description:
In a pioneering treatment, eight-year-old Aditi has been spared from taking life-long drugs to stop her body rejecting her kidney transplant. Doctors at Great Ormond Street Hospital say the breakthrough was made possible by ?reprogramming? Aditi Shankar?s immune system with stem cells before giving her the new kidney.
Because the bone marrow transplant and kidney came from the same donor ? her mother ? the new kidney is working without the need for drugs that stop the body from rejecting a donated organ. Aditi is nowable to swim, sing, dance and play on her trampoline. ?My mum gave me my new blood cells,? said Aditi. ?I got the kidney transplant when I went to special sleep and closed my eyes. Now I have got the line out, I can go swimming.? https://www.theguardian.com/society/2023/sep/22/aditi-shankar-uk-kidney-transplant-great-ormond-street-hospital (originally posted by @GlobalImageServiceCat on 2023-09-23 02:55:30)
item.dc:creator: ThisTimeLastYear

item.title: A Megadump of Music from the '00s Airwaves
item.description:
Green Day - American Idiot (released 20 years ago today in 2004) And a handy dandy Spotify playlist to go with this dump - https://open.spotify.com/playlist/4xdV65TMAzduvJmsTh3MBh?si=zDcSzXwIQU-HeLVT4-HsTg
Kelly Clarkson - Since U Been Gone
Backstreet Boys - Everybody (Back Street's Back)
Missy Elliott (and Ludacris) - Gossip Folks
Vanessa Carlton - A Thousand Miles
Evanescence - Bring Me to Life https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3YxaaGgTQYM
The White Stripes - Seven Nation Army
Avril Lavigne - Sk8er Boi
Outkast - Hey Ya! https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PWgvGjAhvIw
Sheryl Crow - Soak Up the Sun https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KIYiGA_rIls
Christina Aguilera and Lil Kim - Can't Hold Us Down
Beyoncé (and Jay-Z) - Crazy in Love
John Rzeznik - I'm Still Here (from Treasure Planet)
Kelis - Milkshake
Eminem - Lose Yourself
P!nk - Get the Party Started (A Spotify playlist for the collection - https://open.spotify.com/playlist/0kmzsgxV7Oxc1RJVM96ATE ) Wayback in 73 - https://imgur.com/gallery/2rlT8Yh Wayback in 83 - https://imgur.com/gallery/T1CGaYx Wayback in 85 - https://imgur.com/gallery/lrddmXp Wayback in 88 - https://imgur.com/gallery/qBqeSu1 Wayback in 93 - https://imgur.com/gallery/tuAN3R4 Wayback in 95 - https://imgur.com/gallery/KkckKb3 Wayback in 98 - https://imgur.com/gallery/2qki0gA

Missy Elliot - Work It

Alizée - J'en Ai Marre
Beyoncé and Jay-Z - Bonnie and Clyde (featuring the late Lance Reddick)
Kylie Minogue - Spinning Around
Panjabi MC - Mundian To Bach Ke
Ashanti - Rock Wit U (Awww Baby)
U2 - The Hands That Built America
DMX - X Gonna Give it to Ya'
Fallout Boy - Grand Theft Autumn / Where is Your Boy?
No Doubt - It's My Life
Black Eyed Peas - Where is the Love?
Korn - Y'all Want a Single
Justin Timberlake - Cry Me a River
Shakira - Underneath Your Clothes
Michael Bublé - Sway
The Dixie Chicks - Landslide
Lil Kim - Magic Stick
Matchbox Twenty - Unwell
Britney Spears and Madonna - Me Against the Music
Robbie Williams - Come Undone
Linkin Park - Somewhere I Belong
Foo Fighters - Times Like These
Christina Aguilera - Beautiful
Beyoncé (and Sean Paul) - Baby Boy
Wyclef Jean and Missy Elliott - Party To Damascus
Ludacris - Stand Up
Mýa - My Love Is Like... Wo
The Ataris - The Boys of Summer
Elton John (and Blue) - Sorry Seems To Be The Hardest Word
Madonna - Die Another Day
Coldplay - Clocks
Jay-Z - 99 Problems
Clay Aiken - This is the Night
Red Hot Chili Peppers - Can't Stop
Limp Bizkit - Behind Blue Eyes
Alan Jackson and Jimmy Buffett - It's Five O' Clock Somewhere
Sugababes - Hole In The Head
Céline Dion - A New Day Has Come
Sean Paul - Like Glue
Dido - White Flag
Blink-182 - Miss You
The Strokes - Reptilia
Rachel Stevens - Sweet Dreams My L.A. Ex
Delta Goodrem - Lost Without You
Willie Nelson and Toby Keith - Beer For My Horses
The Darkness - I Believe in a Thing Called Love
The Roots - The Seed (2.0)
Queens Of The Stone Age - Go With The Flow
Snoop Dog and Pharrell - Beautiful
Counting Crows (and Vanessa Carlton) - Big Yellow Taxi
Electric Six - Danger! High Voltage!
Busta Rhymes and Mariah Carey - I Know What You Want
Three Days Grace - I Hate Everything About You
Simply Red - Sunrise
Nickleback - Someday
Chevelle - Send the Pain Below
Jason Mraz - The Remedy (I Won't Worry)
Chingy - Right Thurr

item.title: Animal dump. Warning: You will hear a small zebra.
item.description:
nom
A wiggler!
found a cool stick. NO! MY STICK!
Parkour!
frustration
zerb
friends
Careful! Someone is about to play a game of Hungry Hungry Komodo!
3
2
1
A very happy Komodo! Don't worry, the thingie was just a paid actor! He got double scale for this! He's very good!
Another wiggler.
I am not good at this...
a creechur
Heavy is the head that wears the crown
Mum help!
Wheeeee!
Don't drink water afterwards or the fish will go glurbleblellblllurbbble
It is a lion. He eat own ass.
forg
Hicpups
A happy family.
Here is a nice man. He is a silly billy.
Apparently some people like him in a different way. He is the Lumberjack tax.
Here is a nice woman. They have a shared love of violence against logs. She is also the Lumberjack tax. Have a nice day! And do violence to logs!

item.title: Somehow, I really don’t think that this is a good idea…
item.description:
TLDR: The hull of the Titanic is in way too terrible condition to be raised to the surface. . My first post was heavily downvoted, then I ran into some technical difficulties when I tried to post it again. I worked for days on it, so I thought I would make adjustments and try to post it again just one more time. Link to article: https://www.foxnews.com/us/titan-submersible-crew-said-all-good-here-final-messages-moments-before-vessel-imploded-us-coast-guard . THIS POST IS LONG. It goes in depth into why it’s impossible to raise the hull of the Titanic. You have been warned. . . This article is in relation to the current hearings about Ocean Gate's submersible, "Titan" that imploded at the bottom of the ocean last year while on its way to see the Titanic. With the hearings still ongoing and my not being an engineer, I’m in no position to go into depth on that. However, it was the claim that scientists intend to raise the hull of the RMS Titanic that got my attention. I could be wrong, but trying to raise the hull of a ship that has been glued to the bottom of the ocean since 1912 does not seem physically possible. I thought I'd break that down. BUUTTTT…. Unfortunately, there is one ambiguous thing that affects the accuracy of this post: How are they defining "the hull?"
This is the confusing part for me. "The hull" could mean more than one thing. A piece of the hull is possible. However, they didn't say "a piece of the hull," they said "scientists plan to raise the hull.” Raising the full hull is impossible for a few reasons.
Humans can't dive down to the RMS Titanic with scuba gear for a few reasons. Hydrostatic pressure makes it totally impossible, and we'll get into that in a moment. But let's say we have technology to build super fancy scuba gear that can withstand water pressure - it would still be a very bad idea because: 1.) It's too deep 2.) It's too dark 3.) It's too cold 4.) It's too old 5.) It's filled with unknown marine life . DEPTH: The RMS Titanic is 12,500 feet (3,800 meters) below the surface. A scuba diver in a fancy anti-crushing suit would need an insane amount of air tanks just to GET to the wreck, let alone return. . LIGHT: At around 660 feet (200 meters), water has absorbed sunlight, making the area cold and dark. There is *some* light sometimes, but by 6,500 feet (2,000 meters), there is no sunlight at all. Without an adequate amount of light, a diver exploring the Titanic would run a VERY HIGH risk of getting tangled up in rope, rusted metal, etc... and then they'd be trapped and eventually die . WARMTH: Sunlight also means warmth. Without sunlight, the pitch dark parts of the ocean are about 34-36°F (1-2°C). Unless the diver's super fancy anti-crush suit has a heating feature, they would die of hypothermia before they could reach the wreck. . AGE The RMS Titanic is over one hundred years old, and being consumed by iron-eating microbes. A diver swims into an area that's only barely holding up and accidentally hits a weak spot, the whole structure might collapse onto them. . MARINE LIFE Creatures at the deepest depths of the ocean are mostly blind, so they're not going to immediately see you and strike. That doesn't mean that they're docile. We don't know what all is down there, and these unknown creatures might not be happy to see our super tech diver. If the diver is attacked, nobody else could really do anything about it. . With that little thought exercise out of the way, let's go into how the deep ocean can crush a submarine into pieces.
It didn't occur to me until I was making the finishing touches to this post that this comic might be slightly misleading. In a hypothetical situation where a human was simply dropped into the Atlantic ocean to sink to the bottom, they wouldn't be crushed to pieces. Our lungs would collapse, and quickly fill with water - stabilizing our corpse enough to remain in tact. However, a hard inanimate object with compressed air in it would be crushed in the manner I showed in the picture. Now that we've covered hydrostatic pressure, we can move onto the actual shipwrecks.
This is the best infographic I could find with her "stats," but you're going to have to zoom in. This ship was huge.
This is from a documentary that James Cameron made about the wreck of the RMS Titanic. I had to edit out the parts about the stern in order for it to be short enough to post on Imgur. I don’t know how a hydraulic outburst works. I couldn't find much reading material on it. My **GUESS** is that it has to do with water being displaced from the sinking. That water has to go somewhere, and gravity jerks it down to the bottom. That is a guess from someone that is not a phycisist, so don't take that as a fact. Either way, as you can see, the Titanic didn't just softly land at the bottom of the ocean floor. It slammed hard into the sediment, and then a hydraulic outburst smashed it further in like how a hammer bangs a nail into a wooden plank.
This wreck is massive. It is covered in rusticles. Other parts of it are rotting away. For being over a hundred years old in an extremely harsh environment, it's actually well preserved. That doesn't mean that it's in good condition. It's definitely not in any condition to be raised to the surface.
There are multiple things that make raising the bow physically impossible. Now that you've seen how the Titanic slammed into the bottom of the ocean, we can look at the individual factors that will forever keep her there.
This thing is HUGE. You would have to use a lot of heavy equipment to get the bow out of the sediment (provided it's even possible), and that would be unimaginably expensive and possibly environmentally destructive as well.
When the Titanic sank, it didn't just stay in pristine condition at the bottom of the ocean. For all intents and purposes, it became an artificial coral reef for marine life at the bottom of the ocean floor - including bacteria that find iron to be absolutely delicious!
When I saw the 2019 pictures, I was shocked. I wasn't shocked that it was happening, but it's still jarring to see the effects of the halomonas titanicae. The bow has lost so much mass. In fact, it's lost so much mass that it looks like a thin slice of cheese that could collapse at any minute! I would be terrified to go near it!
There will come a time where there won’t be enough metal to hold up the bow. It will eventually collapse into a rust-colored stain on the bottom of the ocean floor, and then dissolve into nothing. That doesn’t have to be a bad thing, though. The RMS Titanic is simply being repurposed. The ocean was there first, and it’s reclaiming a terrible event that led to a massive loss of human life, and creating new marine life from it.
Mostly the reason it would crumble is due to how long it’s been at the bottom of the ocean, and the fact that the actual ship is rapidly decaying due to the bacteria mentioned above. This isn't a brand new wreck. I've heard some mention that the intense water pressure at the bottom of the ocean is the only thing keeping the hull together at this point. I'm not sure if that particular fact is true. Now that we've covered the physical limitations to raising the hull to the surface, there's now the matter of ethics.
A beginning note: In most of the areas of the Titanic, there aren't any physical bodies. Marine life ate them long ago, and their bones dissolved in the saltwater. You CAN tell that a body has been there by their shoes and coats, which were made with a sturdy enough leather to survive the harsh conditions of the Atlantic Ocean. As for actual bodies, Dr. Robert Ballard, who discovered the ship, said he wouldn't be surprised to find well preserved remains deep in the boiler room where it's cut off from ocean currents and marine life. Other scientists have dismissed his claim as impossible. . The debate about the ethics of retrieving artifacts from the Titanic has been a fierce debate since the time of the wreck’s discovery, especially centering on the fact that it is a gravesite. 1,500 people perished in the sinking of the RMS Titanic, and only 337 bodies were ever recovered. The other 1,163 corpses (or at least remnants of the corpses) are with the ship, and hundreds of them are probably deep within the ship. Raising the hull would bring about a massive tangle of ethical and legal issues, because it brings the issue of “when are remnants of human remains defined as a body” back into question. I just don’t see that issue being resolved within a month.
List of Titanic Victims: https://www.discovertitanic.com/titanics-passenger-crew/ Hydrostatic Pressure: https://rwu.pressbooks.pub/webboceanography/chapter/6-1-pressure/ Would The Deep Ocean Crush a Human: https://van.physics.illinois.edu/ask/listing/2266#:~:text=The%20pressure%20from%20the%20water,with%20water%20instead%20of%20air. Halomonas Titanicae: https://ocean.si.edu/ocean-life/bacteria-live-titanic-wreck Source of Titanic’s Deterioration Pictures: https://www.bbc.com/news/science-environment-49420935 Another Article About Titanic’s Deterioration: https://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencetech/article-13803913/Titanic-COLLAPSING-Shocking-photos-reveal.html Titanic Sinking Animation: https://youtu.be/FSGeskFzE0s?si=FaOCGBWZgy0P4CSb Light Traveling in the Ocean: https://oceanexplorer.noaa.gov/facts/light-distributed.html#:~:text=Light%20in%20the%20ocean%20decreases,no%20light%20from%20the%20surface. For anyone interested, this is a post I made four years ago that goes in depth on the sinking of the RMS Titanic: https://imgur.com/gallery/3Zh6KYG I feel like I’m forgetting some sources. I will come back and add them if that’s the case!
item.dc:creator: OlivetheNerd

item.title: Kitten in a Mercedes?
item.description:
It's pretty common, especially in colder areas and colder seasons, cats like to crawl up into snug and warm engines after the people have gone away. If there are stray/feral cats in your area, double check your engine before starting your car in the morning!
Shamelessly stolen from Instagram so you don't have to go there Video credit @theroyaltyautoservice
item.dc:creator: Necridos

item.title: Copy Cats [OC]
item.description:
Last few days of the Animated Pilot Kickstarter! Time to bring out the classics!
The more we raise the better we can make the pilot! We've got a bunch of fun rewards you can only get by backing! https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/litterboxanimation/pilot-episode?ref=f52qpp
BONUS Panel!
item.dc:creator: LitterboxComics
